name: getSignalValueForDescription
title: getSignalValueForDescription
syntax: long getSignalValueForDescription(Signal signal, char description[], int64& rawValue); // form 1
long getSignalValueForDescription(char signalNamel[], char description[], int64& rawValue); // form 2
long getSignalValueForDescription(serviceSignalNumber signal, char description[], int64& rawValue); // form 3
description: Retrieves the value for a value description of a database signal. In this way, you can access the value table of the signal.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: The value description for which the value shall be retrieved.

parameters.name: rawValue
parameters.description: The value for the description. This is the raw value, not the physical value.

parameters.name: Note Signal Ambiguity You have to use further objects to identify the signal uniquely. They are: channel, database name (alias), node name and message name. The exact qualification syntax is:[Channel::][Database name (alias)::][Node::][Message::]Signal The order and completeness of the objects from right to left is important (see example).

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
